items.title: Prefer real over mocked at integration boundaries
items.avoid: Hand-rolled fakes that mimic the upstream's behaviour.
items.prefer: Real Postgres/Redis/Kafka via Docker/testcontainers; real LLM via VCR cassettes.
items.why: Hand-rolled fakes always drift from reality; the whole point of integration tests is exercising the real contract.

items.title: Per-test fresh state
items.avoid: Shared schemas, shared keys, shared topics.
items.prefer: Transactional rollback (SQL), unique namespaces (Redis/queues), per-test temp dirs.
items.why: Order-dependent tests pass solo and fail in parallel — the worst kind of flake.
items.example: Wrap each test in a transaction that rolls back; no global cleanup needed.

items.title: Pin upstream versions
items.why: Upstream releases happen constantly; pinning makes tests reproducible across time.
items.example: Track pin updates in a regular dependency-update PR.

items.title: Separate marker for slow / external suites
items.avoid: All tests in one bucket; everyone running everything every time.
items.prefer: `@pytest.mark.integration` and `@pytest.mark.smoke` with default `-m 'not integration and not smoke'`.
items.why: Local feedback loop stays under 30s; integration runs in CI; smoke runs nightly.

items.title: Investigate flakes — don't just retry
items.prefer: Treat every flake as a bug: race condition, shared state, missing await, real time dependence.
items.why: Retries hide bugs that surface in production at scale.
items.example: A test that 'passes on retry' is the system telling you something is racy. Listen.
